channel.title: Maoism on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Maoism on emsenn.net

item.title: Principal and Secondary Contradiction: Use Cases
item.description:
Mao’s distinction between principal and secondary contradiction is most useful when handled as a stage-specific analytic tool, not as a rigid formula [@mao-on-contradiction-1937].

¶Use case 1: Policy conflict analysis

When multiple policy conflicts coexist, the method asks which contradiction structures the overall field at that stage. Secondary contradictions still matter, but they are interpreted through the principal one.

¶Use case 2: Movement strategy

In movement situations, principal contradiction identifies strategic priority while secondary contradictions identify alliance constraints, internal tensions, and tactical sequencing.

item.title: Principal Contradiction
item.description:
Principal contradiction identifies which contradiction is decisive in a conjuncture when multiple contradictions coexist. In Mao’s formulation, this role can shift as conditions change; principal status is historical, not eternal [@mao-on-contradiction-1937].

This concept is strategic: it orders analytic and political priority.

¶Related terms

  • Secondary Contradiction
  • Contradiction
  • Historicization

item.title: Secondary Contradiction
item.description:
Secondary contradictions are contradictions within a process that do not occupy the decisive position at a given stage. They can intensify, recede, and under changed conditions become principal [@mao-on-contradiction-1937].

Treating secondary contradictions as irrelevant is an error; the concept marks subordination, not insignificance.

¶Related terms

  • Principal Contradiction
  • Contradiction
